paragraphs.en: Homework has never been terribly popular with students and even many parents, but in recent years it has been particularly scorned. School districts across the country, most recently Los Angeles Unified, are revising their thinking on this educational ritual. Unfortunately, L.A. Unified has produced an inflexible policy which mandates that with the exception of some advanced courses, homework may no longer count for more than 10% of a student's academic grade.
paragraphs.zh: 家庭作业从来都不太受学生甚至许多家长的欢迎，但近年来它尤其受到蔑视。全国各地的学区，最近的洛杉矶联合学区，正在修改他们对这种教育仪式的想法。不幸的是，洛杉矶联合大学制定了一项不灵活的政策，规定除某些高级课程外，家庭作业在学生学业成绩中所占的比例不得超过 10%。

paragraphs.en: This rule is meant to address the difficulty that students from impoverished or chaotic homes might have in completing their homework. But the policy is unclear and contradictory. Certainly, no homework should be assigned that students cannot complete on their own or that they cannot do without expensive equipment. But if the district is essentially giving a pass to students who do not do their homework because of complicated family lives, it is going riskily close to the implication that standards need to be lowered for poor children.
paragraphs.zh: 这条规则的目的是解决来自贫困或混乱家庭的学生完成作业的困难。但政策不明确且矛盾。当然，不应该布置学生无法独立完成或没有昂贵设备就无法完成的作业。但如果学区本质上是给那些因为家庭生活复杂而不做作业的学生发放通行证，那么这就很危险地接近于需要降低贫困儿童的标准了。

paragraphs.en: District administrators say that homework will still be a part of schooling; teachers are allowed to assign as much of it as they want. But with homework counting for no more than 10% of their grades, students can easily skip half their homework and see very little difference on their report cards. Some students might do well on state tests without completing their homework, but what about the students who performed well on the tests and did their homework? It is quite possible that the homework helped. Yet rather than empowering teachers to find what works best for their students, the policy imposes a flat, across-the-board rule.
paragraphs.zh: 学区管理人员表示，家庭作业仍将是学校教育的一部分；教师可以根据需要分配任意数量的内容。但由于家庭作业占成绩的比例不超过 10%，学生可以轻松跳过一半的家庭作业，并且在成绩单上看不到什么差别。有些学生可能在没有完成作业的情况下在州考试中取得好成绩，但是那些在考试中表现良好并完成作业的学生呢？家庭作业很可能有帮助。然而，该政策并没有让教师找到最适合学生的方法，而是强加了一条统一的、全面的规则。

paragraphs.en: At the same time, the policy addresses none of the truly thorny questions about homework. If the district finds homework to be unimportant to its students' academic achievement, it should move to reduce or eliminate the assignments, not make them count for almost nothing. Conversely, if homework matters, it should account for a significant portion of the grade. Meanwhile, this policy does nothing to ensure that the homework students receive is meaningful or appropriate to their age and the subject, or that teachers are not assigning more than they are willing to review and correct.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，该政策没有解决有关家庭作业的任何真正棘手的问题。如果学区发现家庭作业对学生的学业成绩并不重要，就应该减少或取消作业，而不是让它们几乎毫无价值。相反，如果家庭作业很重要，它应该占成绩的很大一部分。与此同时，这项政策并不能确保学生收到的家庭作业有意义或适合他们的年龄和科目，或者教师布置的作业不会超出他们愿意复习和纠正的范围。

paragraphs.en: The homework rules should be put on hold while the school board, which is responsible for setting educational policy, looks into the matter and conducts public hearings. It is not too late for L.A. Unified to do homework right.
paragraphs.zh: 在负责制定教育政策的学校董事会调查此事并举行公开听证会期间，家庭作业规则应暂停。对于洛杉矶联合大学来说，做好功课还不算太晚。

questions.text: It is implied in Paragraph 1 that nowadays homework ________
options: is no longer an educational ritual
is not required for advanced courses

questions.text: According to Paragraph 3, one problem with the policy is that it may ________
options: result in students' indifference to their report cards
undermine the authority of state tests

questions.text: As mentioned in Paragraph 4, a key question unanswered about homework 1s whether ________
options: it places extra burdens on teachers

questions.text: A suitable title for this text could be ________
options: A Welcomed Policy for Poor Students
Wrong Interpretations of an Educational Policy

paragraphs.en: Pretty in pink: adult women do not remember being so obsessed with the colour, yet it is pervasive in our young girls' lives. It is not that pink is intrinsically bad, but it is such a tinv slice of the rainbow and, though it may celebrate girlhood in one way, it also repeatedly and firmly fuses girls' identity to appearance. Then it presents that connection, even among two-year-olds, between girls as not only innocent but as evidence of innocence. Looking around, I despaired at the singular lack of imagination about girls' lives and interests.
paragraphs.zh: 漂亮的粉色：成年女性不记得自己对这种颜色如此着迷，但它在我们年轻女孩的生活中却无处不在。这并不是说粉色本质上不好，而是它只是彩虹中的一小部分，尽管它可能以某种方式庆祝少女时代，但它也反复而坚定地将女孩的身份与外表融合在一起。然后，它展示了女孩之间的这种联系，即使是在两岁的孩子之间，不仅是无辜的，而且是无辜的证据。环顾四周，我对女孩们的生活和兴趣缺乏想象力感到绝望。

paragraphs.en: Girls' attraction to pink may seem unavoidable, somehow encoded in their DNA, but according to Jo Paoletti, an associate professor of American Studies, it is not. Children were not colour-coded at all until the early 20th century: in the era before domestic washing machines all babies wore white as a practical matter, since the only way of getting clothes clean was to boil them. What's more, both boys and girls wore what were thought of as gender-neutral dresses. When nursery colours were introduced, pink was actually considered the more masculine colour, a pastel version of red, which was associated with strength. Blue, with its intimations of the Virgin Mary, constancy and faithfulness, symbolised femininity. It was not until the mid-1980s, when amplifying age and sex differences became a dominant children's marketing strategy, that pink fully came into its own, when it began to seem inherently attractive to girls, part of what defined them as female, at least for the first few critical years.
paragraphs.zh: 女孩对粉色的喜爱似乎是不可避免的，不知何故已被编码在她们的 DNA 中，但根据美国研究副教授乔·保莱蒂 (Jo Paoletti) 的说法，事实并非如此。直到20世纪初，孩子们才被用颜色区分：在家用洗衣机还没有出现的时代，所有婴儿实际上都穿白色的衣服，因为清洗衣服的唯一方法就是将它们煮沸。更重要的是，男孩和女孩都穿着被认为是中性的衣服。当引入童趣色时，粉色实际上被认为是更男性化的颜色，是红色的柔和版本，与力量相关。蓝色暗示着圣母玛利亚、坚贞和忠诚，象征着女性气质。直到 20 世纪 80 年代中期，扩大年龄和性别差异成为儿童营销的主要策略时，粉色才完全发挥了作用，它开始对女孩具有天生的吸引力，这也是她们被定义为女性的一部分，至少在最初的关键几年是这样。

paragraphs.en: I had not realised how profoundly marketing trends dictated our perception of what is natural to kids, including our core beliefs about their psychological development. Take the toddler. I assumed that phase was something experts developed after years of research into children's behaviour: wrong. Tums out, according to Daniel Cook, a historian of childhood consumerism, it was popularised as a marketing trick by clothing manufacturers in the 1930s.
paragraphs.zh: 我没有意识到营销趋势如何深刻地决定了我们对孩子自然行为的看法，包括我们对他们心理发展的核心信念。带着小孩。我以为这个阶段是专家们对儿童行为进行多年研究后得出的结论：错误的。研究儿童消费主义的历史学家丹尼尔·库克 (Daniel Cook) 表示，事实证明，这种做法在 20 世纪 30 年代被服装制造商作为一种营销技巧而普及。

paragraphs.en: Trade publications counselled department stores that, in order to increase sales, they should create a "third stepping stone" between infant wear and older kids' clothes. It was only after "toddler" became a common shoppers' term that it evolved into a broadly accepted developmental stage. Splitting kids, or adults, into ever-tinier categories has proved a sure-fire way to boost profits. And one of the easiest ways to segment a market is to magnify gender differences — or invent them where they did not previously exist.
paragraphs.zh: 贸易出版物建议百货公司，为了增加销售额，他们应该在婴儿服装和大童服装之间建立“第三块垫脚石”。直到“幼儿”成为一个常见的购物术语之后，它才演变成一个被广泛接受的发展阶段。事实证明，将儿童或成人分成更小的类别是增加利润的可靠方法。细分市场最简单的方法之一就是放大性别差异，或者在以前不存在的地方创造性别差异。

questions.text: By saying "it is…the rainbow" (Line 3, Para. 1), the author means pink ________
options: should not be associated with girls'innocence
should not be the sole representation of girlhood
cannot influence girls' lives and interests

questions.text: According to Paragraph 2, which of the following is true of colours? ________
options: Blue used to be regarded as the colour for girls.
Pink used to be a neutral colour in symbolising genders.

questions.text: The author suggests that our perception of children's psychological development was much influenced by ________
options: the marketing of products for children

questions.text: We may learn from Paragraph 4 that department stores were advised to ________
options: attach equal importance to different genders
focus on infant wear and older kids'clothes

questions.text: It can be concluded that girls' attraction to pink seems to be — ________
options: clearly explained by their inborn tendency

paragraphs.en: In 2010, a federal judge shook America's biotech industry to its core. Companies had won patents for isolated DNA for decades — by 2005 some 20% of human genes were patented. But in March 2010 a judge ruled that genes were unpatentable. Executives were violently agitated. The Biotechnology Industry Organisation (BIO), a trade group, assured members that this was just a "preliminary step" in a longer battle.
paragraphs.zh: 2010 年，一位联邦法官彻底震撼了美国生物技术产业。数十年来，各家公司都获得了分离 DNA 的专利——到 2005 年，大约 20% 的人类基因获得了专利。但 2010 年 3 月，一位法官裁定基因不可申请专利。高管们非常激动。贸易组织生物技术工业组织（BIO）向成员保证，这只是一场长期斗争中的“初步步骤”。

paragraphs.en: On July 29th they were relieved, at least temporarily. A federal appeals court overturned the prior decision, ruling that Myriad Genetics could indeed hold patents to two genes that help forecast a woman's risk of breast cancer. The chief executive of Myriad, a company in Utah, said the ruling was a blessing to firms and patients alike.
paragraphs.zh: 7 月 29 日，他们松了一口气，至少暂时松了口气。联邦上诉法院推翻了先前的裁决，裁定 Myriad Genetics 确实可以拥有两个有助于预测女性患乳腺癌风险的基因的专利。犹他州 Myriad 公司的首席执行官表示，这一裁决对公司和患者来说都是一件好事。

paragraphs.en: But as companies continue their attempts at personalised medicine, the courts will remain rather busy. The Myriad case itself is probably not over. Critics make three main arguments against gene patents: a gene is a product of nature, so it may not be patented; gene patents suppress innovation rather than reward it; and patents' monopolies restrict access to genetic tests such as Myriad's. A growing number seem to agree. Last year a federal task-force urged reform for patents related to genetic tests. In October the Department of Justice filed a brief in the Myriad case, arguing that an isolated DNA molecule "is no less a product of nature … than are cotton fibres that have been separated from cotton seeds."
paragraphs.zh: 但随着公司继续尝试个性化医疗，法院将仍然相当忙碌。 Myriad 案本身可能还没有结束。批评者对基因专利提出了三个主要论点：基因是自然的产物，因此不能申请专利；基因专利抑制创新而不是奖励创新；专利垄断限制了像 Myriad 那样的基因检测。越来越多的人似乎同意这一观点。去年，一个联邦工作组敦促对与基因测试相关的专利进行改革。 10 月，司法部就 Myriad 案提交了一份简报，认为分离的 DNA 分子“与从棉籽中分离出来的棉花纤维一样，都是自然产物……”。

paragraphs.en: Despite the appeals court's decision, big questions remain unanswered. For example, it is unclear whether the sequencing of a whole genome violates the patents of individual genes within it. The case may yet reach the Supreme Court.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管上诉法院做出了裁决，但仍有一些重大问题没有得到解答。例如，目前尚不清楚整个基因组的测序是否侵犯了其中单个基因的专利。该案可能仍会提交最高法院。

paragraphs.en: As the industry advances, however, other suits may have an even greater impact. Companies are unlikely to file many more patents for human DNA molecules — most are already patented or in the public domain. Firms are now studying how genes interact, looking for correlations that might be used to determine the causes of disease or predict a drug's efficacy. Companies are eager to win patents for "connecting the dots," explains Hans Sauer, a lawyer for the BIO.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，随着行业的进步，其他诉讼可能会产生更大的影响。公司不太可能为人类 DNA 分子申请更多专利——大多数已经获得专利或属于公共领域。公司现在正在研究基因如何相互作用，寻找可用于确定疾病原因或预测药物疗效的相关性。 BIO 律师汉斯·索尔 (Hans Sauer) 解释说，公司渴望赢得“连接点”的专利。

paragraphs.en: Their success may be determined by a suit related to this issue, brought by the Mayo Clinic, which the Supreme Court will hear in its next term. The BIO recently held a convention which included sessions to coach lawyers on the shifting landscape for patents. Each meeting was packed.
paragraphs.zh: 他们的成功可能取决于梅奥诊所提起的与此问题相关的诉讼，最高法院将在下个任期审理该诉讼。 BIO 最近举行了一次会议，其中包括对律师进行关于专利格局变化的培训课程。每次会议都座无虚席。

questions.text: It can be learned from Paragraph 1 that the biotech companies would like ________
options: the BIO to issue a warning
judges to rule out gene patenting

questions.text: Those who are against gene patents believe that ________
options: patents on genes depend much on innovation
courts should restrict access to genetic tests

questions.text: According to Hans Sauer, companies are eager to win patents for ________
By saying "Each meeting was packed" (Line 4, Para. 6), the author means that ________
options: the BIO was a powerful organisation
gene patenting was a great concern
lawyers were keen to attend conventions

questions.text: Generally speaking, the author's attitude toward gene patenting is ________

paragraphs.en: The great recession may be over, but this era of high joblessness is probably beginning. Before it ends, it will likely change the life course and character of a generation of young adults. And ultimately, it is likely to reshape our politics, our culture, and the character of our society for years.
paragraphs.zh: 大衰退可能已经结束，但这个高失业率的时代可能正在开始。在它结束之前，它很可能会改变一代年轻人的生命历程和性格。最终，它可能会在未来几年重塑我们的政治、文化和社会特征。

paragraphs.en: No one tries harder than the jobless to find silver linings in this national economic disaster. Many said that unemployment, while extremely painful, had improved them in some ways: they had become less materialistic and more financially prudent; they were more aware of the struggles of others. In limited respects, perhaps the recession will leave society better off. At the very least, it has awoken us from our national fever dream of easy riches and bigger houses, and put a necessary end to an era of reckless personal spending.
paragraphs.zh: 没有人比失业者更努力地在这场国家经济灾难中寻找一线希望。许多人表示，失业虽然极其痛苦，但在某些方面改善了他们：他们变得不那么物质主义，在财务上更加谨慎；他们更了解其他人的挣扎。在有限的方面，经济衰退也许会让社会变得更好。至少，它让我们从热衷于轻松致富和拥有更大房子的全国性梦想中醒来，并结束了不计后果的个人消费时代。

paragraphs.en: But for the most part, these benefits seem thin, uncertain, and far off. In The Moral Consequences of Economic Growth, the economic historian Benjamin Friedman argues that both inside and outside the U.S., lengthy periods of economic sta g nation or decline have almost alway s left society more mean­ spirited and less inclusive, and have usually stopped or reversed the advance of rights and freedoms. Anti-immigrant sentiment typically increases, as does conflict between races and classes.
paragraphs.zh: 但在大多数情况下，这些好处似乎微不足道、不确定且遥远。经济历史学家本杰明·弗里德曼在《经济增长的道德后果》一书中指出，无论是在美国国内还是国外，长期的经济停滞或衰退几乎总是会让社会变得更加刻薄、包容性更差，并且通常会阻止或逆转权利和自由的进步。反移民情绪通常会增加，种族和阶级之间的冲突也会增加。

paragraphs.en: Income inequality usually falls during a recession, but it has not shrunk in this one. Indeed, this period of economic weakness may reinforce class divides, and decrease opportunities to cross them — especially for young people. The research of Till Von Wachter, the economist at Columbia University, suggests that not all people graduatin g into a recession see their life chances dimmed: those with deg rees from elite universities catch up fairly quickly to where they otherwise would have been if they had graduated in better times; it is the masses beneath them that are left behind.
paragraphs.zh: 收入不平等通常会在经济衰退期间缩小，但在这次衰退中并没有缩小。事实上，这段经济疲软时期可能会加剧阶级鸿沟，并减少跨越鸿沟的机会——尤其是对年轻人而言。哥伦比亚大学经济学家蒂尔·冯·瓦赫特 (Till Von Wachter) 的研究表明，并非所有在经济衰退中毕业的人都会发现自己的生活机会变得黯淡：那些拥有精英大学学位的人会很快赶上他们如果在更好的时期毕业的话本可以达到的水平；被抛在后面的是他们下面的群众。

paragraphs.en: In the Internet ag e, it is particularly easy to see the resentment that has always been hidden within American society. More difficult, in the moment, is discerning precisely how these lean times are affectin g society 's character. In many respects, the U.S. was more socially tolerant entering this recession than at any time in its history, and a variety of national polls on social conflict since then have shown mixed results. We will have to wait and see exactly how these hard times will reshape our social fabric. But they certainly will reshape it, and all the more so the longer they extend.
paragraphs.zh: 在网络时代，我们特别容易看到美国社会中一直隐藏的怨恨情绪。目前，更困难的是准确地辨别这些经济不景气的时期是如何影响社会特征的。从许多方面来看，美国在进入这场衰退时比历史上任何时候都更加宽容，而且自那时以来有关社会冲突的各种全国民意调查显示出不同的结果。我们将不得不拭目以待，看看这些困难时期将如何重塑我们的社会结构。但他们肯定会重塑它，而且随着时间的延长，情况更是如此。

questions.text: By saying "to find silver linings" (Line 1, Para. 2) the author suggests that the jobless try to ________
options: make profits from the troubled economy
look on the bright side of the recession

questions.text: According to Paragraph 2, the recession has made people ________
Benjamin Friedman believes that economic recessions may ________
options: impose a heavier burden on immigrants
bring out more evils of human nature
promote the advance of rights and freedoms
ease conflicts between races and classes

questions.text: The research of Till Von Wachter suggests that in the recession graduates from elite universities tend to ________
options: lag behind the others due to decreased opportunities
catch up quickly with experienced employees
see their life chances as dimmed as the others'
recover more quickly than the others

questions.text: The author thinks that the influence of hard times on society is ________
